scope.name: ATAO_STRUCTURAL_VALIDATION
scope.expansion: Agent Tool Action Object structural validation
scope.purpose: Define the deterministic structural checks that classify an ATAO candidate as VALID_STRUCTURE or NULL before authority binding, AEO materialization, validation-state transition, execution, or proof generation can occur.

structural_object_model.root_schema_object_type_policy: Root public ATAO schemas may omit object_type for backward compatibility, but runtime legitimacy schema validation requires object_type=ATAO.

atao_id.purpose: Stable candidate identifier only; not an authority token.

agent_id.purpose: Captures proposing agent identity only; does not grant authority.

session_id.purpose: References an external session lineage candidate; structural presence does not prove active session continuity.

scope.purpose: Bounds the proposed action; structural validity requires object shape but does not adjudicate semantic sufficiency.

timestamp.purpose: Candidate observation time; freshness and replay windows require later governance checks.

validation_rules.requirement: Input must be a JSON object, not an array, scalar, or malformed JSON.
All required fields for the applicable ATAO schema profile must be present.
No undeclared root or proposed_action fields may be present.
All non-empty string fields must contain at least one character after JSON parsing.
risk_class must be one of P0, P1, P2, or P3.
timestamp must parse as a date-time string.
Hash- or merkle_root-suffixed fields, if admitted by a future schema revision, must be lowercase 64-character hex strings.

VALID_STRUCTURE_means: The ATAO candidate satisfies deterministic structural requirements.
The candidate can be considered for subsequent authority binding or AEO materialization by separate governed logic.
VALID_STRUCTURE_does_not_mean: the proposed action is semantically safe
valid_structure_semantics.NULL_means: The candidate is structurally invalid or ambiguous and must not advance to authority binding or execution eligibility.

replay_boundaries.replay_semantics: Structural validation is replay-neutral. Replay resistance must be enforced by later governed stages that bind exact object hash, authority, invocation nonce, and proof lineage.
